content.assessment: This proposal most directly engages the equity lens's core questions by pairing closure with binding transport guarantees, disadvantage compensation, and non-discrimination catchment review — explicitly naming the segregation-reproduction risk that consolidation could create at destination schools. Given that Hungary already exhibits the largest urban-rural outcome gap among OECD countries and stark Roma/non-Roma disparities in early leaving, the statutory catchment-review safeguard is a necessary but unproven condition; its success depends entirely on implementation rigor not guaranteed by the mechanism description alone.
opportunities: Targeted disadvantage compensation could be calibrated specifically to the documented gap in early school leaving between Roma and non-Roma populations (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-08)
Could correct the current drift toward under-resourced church transfer as a segregation-avoidance workaround by offering a transparent, rights-based alternative (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03)
strengths: Explicitly requires non-discrimination catchment review for consolidated destination schools, directly responding to documented segregation risk and legal precedent (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-04, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-05)
Statutory transport guarantees target the documented disproportionate burden of long commutes on lower-income households (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-10)

Recognizes zero-enrollment cliff cases as demographic facts distinct from contested viability debates, avoiding false equivalence with villages where closure is genuinely a policy choice (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-01)
threats: Consolidated destination schools risk reproducing the territorial concentration of disadvantage already documented in North-East Hungary if catchment design is not rigorously enforced (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-05)
Teacher shortages and aging workforce concentrated in rural areas may simply relocate to destination schools without dedicated staffing remedies (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-09)
weaknesses: Even with mitigation, consolidation could still relocate sorting to destination schools if catchment review is weak or under-resourced, especially given church-sector schools' already more advantaged intake as an uncontrolled alternative absorbing displaced pupils (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-04)

Transport guarantees address frequency and route gaps but the underlying commute-length equity harm persists structurally for consolidated destinations, only partially offset by 2023 free transport for under-14s
